sound_type: cub distress call -- high mewling squall 'meeew, meeew'. 300-800 Hz. Separation from sow. Summer. E=4
ecosystem: Forest, meadow, berry patch. Summer. First summer with sow. Cub following mother.
season: Summer (May-September). First summer after emergence from den.
family: Ursidae (third and final entry -- huff at 200, jaw-pop at 201)

coordinates.description: Black Bear throughout western Washington. Forest and meadow. Cub with sow in summer.

taxonomy_note.description: Third and final Black Bear entry. Bear trio complete (200-202). Final Ursidae in the engine.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Ursidae: Black Bear Huff (200, E=5), Black Bear Jaw-Pop (201, E=6), Black Bear Cub Distress (202, E=4). COMPLETE.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Ursus from Latin 'bear'. americanus for American continent.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Ursus -- bears. Only Ursus species in PNW lowlands.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Third and final Carnivora Ursidae entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Ursidae: third and final entry. Bear trio complete.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 300-800 Hz. High for a bear. Produced by small vocal cords.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Distress mew: 0.5-2.0 sec. Repeated every 2-5 seconds.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single mewing vocalization. Repeated insistently when distressed.
song_metrics.note_types: Distress mew (high, thin, separation), contentment hum (low, nursing).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Repeated mews at regular intervals. Rate increases with distress level. Continuous when panicked.
song_metrics.timbre: Thin, high, mewling. Small body cavity. Small vocal cords. Not yet the resonant adult voice.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Infant vulnerability. Audible at 50-150 m. Sufficient for sow. Not long-range.
song_metrics.tempo: Mew every 2-5 seconds. Faster when more distressed. Continuous when panicked.
song_metrics.context: Separation from sow. Fear. Tree-climbing failure. First summer. Close to mother.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Cub distress calls are structurally consistent across populations.
song_metrics.key_property: CUB DISTRESS. High mewling squall. 300-800 Hz. E=4. BEAR TRIO 3/3 (200-202) COMPLETE. Final Ursidae. INVERSION from adult E=6 to infant E=4. v1.49.343.

pelage.overall: Cub: black fur, lighter muzzle. Small (2-4 kg at emergence, growing to 15-25 kg by fall). Claws for climbing.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Small size. Black fur. Lighter muzzle. Often seen with sow (larger adult). Climbing behavior.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Born nearly hairless. Full coat by emergence. Grows rapidly through summer.

physical_description.overall: Cub at emergence: 2-4 kg (5-10 lbs). By fall: 15-25 kg (35-55 lbs). Black fur. Small claws for climbing.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Small bear. Always with or near sow. Climbs trees when threatened. Mews when separated.
physical_description.field_id: Small black bear (cub) near larger adult (sow). If you see a cub, the sow is nearby. Leave immediately. Do not approach.

nodes.title: Bear Trio Complete -- 200-202
nodes.description: Three entries, one species. Huff (awareness, E=5), jaw-pop (threat, E=6), cub distress (need, E=4). Escalation then inversion. Final Ursidae.

nodes.title: Cub Distress Call -- The Sound of Need
nodes.description: 300-800 Hz. High, thin, mewling. Repeated every 2-5 seconds. The vocal tether between cub and sow. Brings the sow running.

nodes.title: Maternal Response -- Highest Risk Encounter
nodes.description: Sow responds to cub distress with defensive aggression. The most dangerous bear encounter for hikers. Leave immediately.

nodes.title: Infant Contact Call -- Bear Cub vs. Deer Fawn
nodes.description: Cub distress (E=4, 300-800 Hz) vs. fawn bleat (E=3, 500-1,500 Hz). Both infant calls. Cubs more vocal, more insistent.

cross_references.relationship: BEAR TRIO 1/3! Huff (E=5). Trio begins with adult awareness. Cub inverts at E=4.
BEAR TRIO 2/3! Jaw-pop (E=6) to cub (E=4). The -2 inversion. Intimidation to vulnerability.
INFANT PARALLEL! Fawn (E=3) and cub (E=4). Both infant contact calls. Cubs more vocal.
ECHO AND CALLING! Reverb (coast echo) and cub mew (forest echo). Searching sounds.
